items.title: [正面] I also play on google play store on my phone, and ...
items.summary:
I also play on google play store on my phone, and this is my entire personal experience.
Before coming to this game I played Reverse 1999 for months (Not on steam) and when I found this game on the play store and decided to get it. This game was just about everything and more I expected the game to be.
Some things I found while playing this game:

1. The graphics and character designs are top tier. They are all unique and all just look amazing.

2. This game was well put together to not feel like just another gacha fighting game. There are so many mechanics you can explore in this game, which makes the game feel like it's worth your time to play and makes it funner.

3. The gacha system is better than what I have ever seen before, literally it only takes 30 pulls to guarantee a SSR, and even then you can get a SSR before then. Plus, all SSRs will eventually become apart of the permanent banner, in which you can get the character anytime with just a little luck.

4. This game does not disappoint when it comes down to story idea. The whole idea was spectacular and easily draws you in to want to hear more, its like a good book that you just don't want to stop reading.

5. The fighting style is somewhat easy to learn, however you'll get the hang of it eventually.

6. However, even though it's worth your time, I do not recommend spending money at least when you start the game. I suggest doing it later in the game. 

7. This game is very much similar to Reverse: 1999, so if you like that game then I'm sure you'd like this one too!

8. This game sadly has only very few translations and uses GPT for languages like English, and the voice over is in Japanese.

9. The game doesn't take much storage up in your device and downloading is minimal and very quick.

(Thank you for reading my review! :D )

items.title: [正面] really enjoy this game. it is super F2P friendly a...
items.summary: really enjoy this game. it is super F2P friendly and if you enjoy FGO or even HSR i really do suggest this game. some of the translations are funky, but honestly really not bad. i do wish they didn't use AI for the translations though...

items.title: [正面] I love this game, please give it a try if you like...
items.summary:
I love this game, please give it a try if you liked the trailer.

>Pros, consider playing if you:

1) Liked R1999. Similar play and layout, except for within each game level you will "explore" and encounter events that can give you different boosts before you face the boss. Morimens has heavier dark themes in comparison to R1999 though, so beware (eg. Body horror, su!cide, etc)

2) Like team building and strategy. The characters you collect are all categorised into 4 realms (chaos, aequor, caro, ultra), and each one have their own unique shtick that influences your gameplay. You can mix and match up to 2 realms at once, Aequor is highly reccomended for newbies, but there is a tutorial section where you can play each standard character and realm before you decide to stick to one.

3) Just want something to log into, complete your dailies and ciao while occasionally investing a bit more time to complete events. Low commitment after you understand what are the dailies and weeklies you should prioritise.

>Avoid if you: 

1) Are sensitive to dark themes. Rarely visually/audible explicit, but definitely to look out for though the game does add reminders on the especially dark chapters. Don't play and then start clutching pearls, read the sign DEAD DOVE DO NOT OPEN!

2) Are impatient/Have too much free time/Expecting a game where you can constantly do stuff 24/7 to rank-up, because you WILL be timegated by your account level occassionally when hit about level 35+, just take it slow.

3) Cannot stand automated translations. I'm familiar with reading CN novel machine translations, so I recognise most of the phrases that are confusing in the game are only because of direct translation. So if you try playing, please keep in mind the source language.

>Translation matter: 
It's a gacha game with an official EN translation in the works (estimated release in late 2025) from CN, but frankly I just looked up a newbies guide on YT for the first day and after that I knew what's what. I really dont find the translation an issue except for when the terminologies were inconsistent, but even then it doesn't really effect my understanding personally.

*Did I mention this game rewards you with pulls like candy? Mostly standard banner pulls + 3 free standard character selectors for newbies, but plenty good since many standard charas can last you.
